message: spends his first day of leave in silence and alone by his tent, coming to terms with what has gone on in the previous few days. Traitors in camp, flogging, walls collapsing, and pigs using swords? Images race through his mind of what he has seen.
eventually gets up, takes his weapons and heads over to the training courtyard. Looking for people to spar and train with.

The training yard has a handful of veterans, exercising, and trading blows. A few younger and greener mercenaries watch or sometimes join in. All too often they leave with bruised arms and legs, as well as bruised pride.
watches the veterans and the new recruits. He walks over to a group of the younger ones who look a bit beaten up.
looks up at Biron, a little of their surliness lost by the opportunity for training from an officer.
When fighting you shouldn't be closing yourself up, you're to easy to be pushed off balance.
picks the kid up by the scruff and positions him.
Chris, make a "martial weapon" check (Strength with proficiency bonus).
You don't want to know what would've happened if you;d rolled iunder 5, so that wasn't a totally bad roll.
Well he can't roll under 5, can he? it's all ways 6 or higher 😛
Roll a nat 1 woulda screwed me haha.
Ha true, didn't think about that!

Biron whiles away a few days attempting to spar and train some of the younger mercenaries. It's good, honest work but they're either too workshy or flighty, and it's a week wasted. Their skills do not improve and no-one seems to recognise what you are doing.
